description: Figure 11 is a stacked bar chart that compares the experimentally measured yield strengths with the calculated values derived from four additive strengthening mechanisms for Fe–20Mn–9Al–1.5C austenitic low-density steels alloyed with Ni and Cr. The x-axis lists four alloy compositions—3Cr, 2Ni, 2Ni3Cr, and 2Ni5Cr—while the y-axis reports yield strength in MPa. Each bar is divided into four color-coded segments: σ₀ (friction stress, orange), σgb (grain boundary strengthening, green), σss (solid solution strengthening, purple), and σprep (precipitation strengthening, yellow). Brown circular markers indicate the experimental yield strengths determined from tensile tests. Visually, σss provides the largest contribution (approximately 500–560 MPa) across all samples, whereas σprep varies markedly, being highest for 2Ni and lower for 3Cr, 2Ni3Cr, and 2Ni5Cr. The sum of the calculated contributions closely matches the experimental data points, validating the analytical superposition model. The figure supports the core conclusion that solid solution strengthening is the dominant mechanism due to the high 1.5% C content, while Ni and Cr synergistically control precipitation strengthening by altering precipitate type and size (κ-carbides versus LRO domains), thereby enabling targeted strength optimization in these lightweight steels.
